text: CENTCOM BRIEFING: Axios reports Adm. Brad Cooper briefing Trump TODAY on 3 military options: (1) 'short and powerful' wave of strikes on Iranian infrastructure, (2) plan to seize part of Hormuz potentially with ground forces, (3) special forces raid to grab Iran's HEU at Isfahan. This is why Brent just hit $126.
verificationNote: Axios scoop confirmed. JPost, CNBC citing same report. Oil price spike correlates.

text: BRENT $126.10 — NEW WARTIME HIGH. Blew through $125 resistance on CENTCOM briefing report. +57.9% from pre-war $79.84. WTI $108.51. Goldman says Hormuz exports at 4% of normal. If Trump greenlights any of the 3 CENTCOM options, we're looking at $140+ easily.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed $126.10. Goldman Sachs 4% figure from their energy desk note.

text: HEZBOLLAH DRONE ATTACK: Explosive drone from Lebanon strikes near Shomera in Western Galilee. Vehicle ablaze, fire department responding. Interceptor fired at 2nd suspicious aerial target — results under review. Fiber-optic FPV drones immune to Israeli EW jamming confirmed as major capability gap.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog April 30. Al Jazeera fiber-optic drone analysis.

text: IRAN WEAPON THREAT: Navy commander Rear Adm. Shahram Irani says Iran will confront enemies 'very soon' with weapon they're 'deeply afraid of' — 'could give them a heart attack.' No specifics. Could be bluff, could be anti-ship ballistic missile, could be nuclear. Timing with CENTCOM briefing is notable.
verificationNote: Press TV, India TV News confirmed statement. Interpretation speculative.

text: IAEA: Grossi says JCPOA 'cannot constitute a basis' for new Iran nuclear deal. Estimates ~200kg HEU still at Isfahan in tunnels. IAEA cannot verify — inspections ended after June 2025 attack. This is exactly what the CENTCOM HEU seizure option is targeting.
verificationNote: UN News, Foreign Policy confirmed Grossi statements at NPT review.

text: DAY 62 LATE PT ROUNDUP: Brent $126.10 wartime high (+57.9%). CENTCOM briefs Trump on 3 military options. Gas $4.23 wartime high. Israeli double-tap kills 5 in Lebanon. Hezbollah drone hits Shomera. Iran threatens new weapon. IAEA: JCPOA 'no basis.' $344M Tether frozen. War Powers deadline TOMORROW.
verificationNote: CNBC, Axios, NBC News, Euronews, Times of Israel, Press TV, UN News. All cross-verified.

text: BLACK WEDNESDAY: At least 28 killed across 23 villages in southern Lebanon — Sultanieh, Qalaouiyeh, Yohmor, Zawtar al-Sharqiya, Meifdoun, Biyyout al-Saiyad, Kunin all hit. IDF Arabic spokesman ordered evacuations within 1km of any Hezbollah personnel. Lebanese health min: 9 killed at one site incl. 2 children.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera 'Black Wednesday' analysis confirmed scale and locations. Cross-verified by Times of Israel liveblog and L'Orient Today.

text: OIL SHARP REVERSAL: Brent crashed from intraday $126 to $114.66 — a -$11.44 / -9.1% pullback in hours. WTI eased to $105.38 from $110.90 peak. Profit-taking + reports CENTCOM brief is preliminary, not strike-imminent. Volatility extreme. Range trading $100-$120 likely until War Powers May 1 resolved.
verificationNote: CNBC, CNN Business, TradingEconomics confirmed pullback. Babypips charted intraday range.

text: DARK EAGLE REQUEST: CENTCOM has formally requested deployment of Army's Long-Range Hypersonic Weapon (Dark Eagle) to the Middle East — first potential combat use of US hypersonic missile globally. Range ~1,725mi, Mach 5, ~$15M each. Only ~8 in inventory; never officially combat-certified. Iran moved launchers out of PrSM range.
verificationNote: Bloomberg scoop. Times of Israel + Army Recognition cited same. Pentagon has not officially confirmed deployment.

text: MOJTABA SURFACES: Mojtaba Khamenei issues Persian Gulf Day message — 'new chapter,' 'future without America' in Hormuz, vows to protect nuclear/missile capabilities as 'national assets.' Hours after Rubio confirmed US has 'indications' Mojtaba is alive. Succession messaging unmistakable.
verificationNote: PressTV (official), The Week India, ANI News confirmed statement. Rubio's Hill comments separately confirmed.

text: HEGSETH SENATE GRILLING: ~6 hours of testimony. Pentagon comptroller Jules Hurst confirms $25B war cost (CNN: actual $40-50B with base repairs). White House requesting $1.5T defense budget — biggest since WWII. Hegseth's claim that 60-day War Powers clock 'pauses' during ceasefire is contested by most legal scholars.
verificationNote: WaPo, PBS NewsHour, Euronews, CSMonitor confirmed hearing details. Defense Post & NBC confirmed $25B figure.

text: DAY 63 MIDDAY ROUNDUP: 'Black Wednesday' kills 28 in 23 S. Lebanon villages. Hezbollah drone wounds 12 IDF at Shomera. CENTCOM briefs Trump on 3 options + Dark Eagle hypersonic request. USS Gerald Ford departing Mideast (carrier → 2). Hegseth 6hr Senate hearing — $25B confirmed. Brent retreats to $114.66 after $126 spike. Gas $4.30. UAE OPEC exit TOMORROW. War Powers TOMORROW.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Axios, Bloomberg, WaPo, CNBC, NPR, AAA. All cross-verified.

text: BREAKING: Sgt. Liem Ben Hamo (19) of Golani Brigade Battalion 13 killed by Hezbollah explosive drone in Qantara village, S. Lebanon. Second drone intercepted. Another soldier moderately wounded. 19th IDF fatality. Hezbollah fiber-optic drones proving devastating — 1 killed, 13 wounded in single day.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post, Haaretz, JNS, Israel National News confirmed identity and details.

text: WAR POWERS DEADLINE ARRIVES: Senate blocked 6th War Powers vote 47-50. Collins (R-ME) broke with GOP — first time. Paul (R-KY) also voted for. Hegseth's claim that ceasefire 'pauses' the 60-day clock is legally questionable per most constitutional scholars. Murkowski (R-AK) says AUMF coming if no plan.
verificationNote: Time, Maine Public, WaPo, Foreign Policy confirmed vote tally and Collins break. CNN confirmed Murkowski AUMF plan.

text: MARITIME FREEDOM CONSTRUCT: US launches new joint State Dept/CENTCOM initiative to build international coalition for Hormuz reopening. Embassies instructed to approach partner nations by May 1. UK/France leading parallel 30-nation effort. This is the diplomatic track parallel to CENTCOM's 3 military options.
verificationNote: Euronews, ABC News confirmed MFC launch. Washington Times confirmed UK/France 30-nation effort.

text: OIL PULLBACK DEEPENS: Brent $108.82 in May 1 overnight trading — down from $114.01 close and $126 wartime high. -$17.28 from peak in 24 hours. WTI $104.84. Profit-taking + diplomatic overtures (Maritime Freedom Construct, Putin warning Trump). But fundamentals unchanged — Hormuz remains closed.
verificationNote: CNBC, Investing.com confirmed price levels. Reuters confirmed pullback dynamics.

text: IRGC ESCALATION: Aerospace Commander Mousavi threatens strikes on US bases AND warships — 'we've seen what happened to your regional bases, we will see the same thing happen to your warships.' This is new — Hezbollah drones proving effective against IDF; IRGC signaling similar capabilities vs. US Navy assets.
verificationNote: Siasat.com confirmed Mousavi statement. Cross-referenced with IRGC Navy Irani's 'weapon' threat from earlier today.

text: DAY 63 EVENING PT ROUNDUP: IDF Sgt. Ben Hamo KIA in Qantara drone strike. Senate rejects War Powers 47-50 — Collins breaks with GOP. Brent retreats to $108.82 from $126 wartime high. Maritime Freedom Construct launched. IRGC threatens US warships. IAEA Grossi: old deal 'no basis.' Putin warns Trump. UAE OPEC exit NOW IN EFFECT. Hormuz Day 60. War Powers May 1 (TODAY).
verificationNote: JPost, Time, CNBC, Euronews, Siasat, FP, Al Jazeera. All cross-verified.
